basics.name: Waldo Ibarra
basics.label: Hands-On Software Architect | AI Engineer
basics.summary:
Software Architect with 11+ years building scalable SaaS platforms, APIs, and cloud infrastructure — now specializing in applied AI engineering.

Hands-on with RAG pipelines, LLM agents, QLoRA fine-tuning, and production eval frameworks, alongside deep expertise in TypeScript, Node.js, React, Python, AWS, Docker, and IaC.

Proven leading ~55-person engineering orgs while staying close to the code; strong in Clean Architecture, DDD, and performance-driven system design.

work.name: Uniko
work.summary: Built SaaS platforms, owning the full backend stack from API design to cloud infrastructure.
highlights: Architected and shipped multiple scalable SaaS APIs in TypeScript/Node.js using NestJS, designed for high availability and long-term maintainability.
Designed AWS-based cloud infrastructure with automated CI/CD pipelines, reducing manual deployment overhead and standardizing release quality.
Optimized PostgreSQL queries and introduced Redis caching layer, improving API response times and lowering operational costs.
Established NestJS architecture patterns and coding standards, enabling consistent, scalable development across the codebase.

work.name: Spark Cooperative
work.summary: Built a full-stack template management system that eliminated manual YAML workflows, replacing script-based processes with a self-serve UI editor.
highlights: Architected and shipped a UI-driven template editor that eliminated manual YAML file creation and script execution, significantly reducing developer setup time.
Delivered a full-stack solution in TypeScript across a Node.js/React frontend and MongoDB backend, built for maintainability and scalability from day one.

work.name: WGSN
work.summary: Short-term contract at a global trend forecasting platform, focused on AI-driven personalization, architecture standardization, and engineering enablement.
highlights: Built an AI-driven recommendation engine delivering user-specific content based on item interaction signals.
Standardized architecture design and documentation practices using Event Storming and C4 model, improving team alignment and system clarity.
Automated infrastructure provisioning and development workflows using Terraform and CI/CD pipelines, reducing manual ops overhead.
Mentored engineers on clean code principles, design patterns, and automated testing, raising the team's baseline engineering standards.

work.name: Guros
work.summary: Joined as founding engineer and grew into leading the full engineering org — ultimately overseeing 50+ engineers, 8 tech leads, and 3 architects across a multi-product SaaS ecosystem.
highlights: Architected and scaled a SaaS API from scratch using Serverless Architecture, DDD Layered Architecture, and Ports & Adapters pattern.
Scaled and led an engineering org of 50+ engineers, 8 tech leads, and 3 architects, driving system design, mentoring, code reviews, and hiring.
Designed an evolutionary architecture across the product ecosystem focused on scalability, testability, and observability.
Established Agile and TDD practices as the engineering foundation, improving delivery consistency and code quality.
Delivered internal talks on C4 model documentation, clean code, design patterns, and DevOps practices org-wide.

work.name: Fanbot
work.summary: Built and improved a Facebook Messenger chatbot platform, delivering new features and an embedded interactive experience to drive user engagement.
highlights: Developed and shipped new conversational features for a Facebook Messenger chatbot using Dialogflow, improving conversation flow and user retention.
Integrated an embedded React-based minigame into the chatbot experience, increasing user interaction depth and session length.
Refactored the core codebase for modularity, reducing coupling and enabling faster feature iteration.

work.name: Paystand
work.summary: Full-stack engineer on a B2B payments platform, delivering microservices, a monolith-to-microservices migration, and compliance-grade data tooling.
highlights: Migrated core components from a monolithic architecture to microservices, improving system flexibility, scalability, and independent deployability.
Built a PCI-compliant data import script to securely migrate bank and card payment data from Authorize.net, ensuring regulatory compliance.
Implemented multi-schema search with Elasticsearch, significantly improving search performance and user experience across the platform.
Developed accounts receivable and payable microservices, expanding core payment features for B2B clients.

work.name: H-E-B México
work.summary: Contract engagement across architecture and backend engineering for a major retail company, delivering high-performance APIs, search infrastructure, and internal tooling adopted org-wide.
highlights: Reduced e-commerce search response time by 90% by implementing Elasticsearch with optimized search algorithms, directly improving customer experience and platform engagement.
Built a Node.js ETL synchronization system between MongoDB and MSSQL, improving data accuracy and cross-system integration.
Designed and built high-performance Node.js APIs, improving system efficiency across multiple internal teams.
Automated development workflows using Shell scripts, Node.js CLIs, and Jenkins pipelines, reducing manual operational overhead.
Directed POC projects for CA API Gateway, RedHat OpenShift, Docker, and Jenkins to guide informed technology adoption.
Led security compliance initiatives for internal APIs, strengthening security posture and meeting industry standards.

work.name: Waspe
work.summary: Full-stack engineer on a multi-product ERP ecosystem, delivering microservices, system integrations, compliance features, and a 95% reduction in deployment time.
highlights: Reduced deployment time by 95% by implementing CI/CD automation with Gulp and GitHub webhooks, enabling faster and more reliable releases.
Implemented real-time bi-directional data sync between the in-house ERP and SAP B1 via a Node.js microservice, improving data accuracy and operational efficiency.
Delivered Peru's SUNAT-compliant electronic invoicing integration into the ERP, ensuring regulatory compliance.
Built Node.js microservices for mail delivery and push notifications via FCM, improving system reliability and user engagement.
Developed a centralized download and update hub for desktop applications, streamlining feature delivery across the user base.

work.name: Epicor Software
work.summary: Software engineering internship at an enterprise ERP company, contributing full-stack features and internal service integrations.
highlights: Delivered new features and live-editing capabilities for a web ERP system using VB.NET and jQuery.
Integrated multiple internal services into the VB.NET monolith, improving system cohesion and operational efficiency.

volunteer.summary: Contributing to open source projects to improve software quality and give back to the community.
highlights: Fixed bugs and added new features.

education.summary:
Ongoing independent study focused on applied engineering and emerging technologies.

**▪** System design, Clean architecture, and Domain-Driven Design (DDD)

**▪** Cloud infrastructure: Docker, Terraform, CI/CD, AWS

**▪** Applied AI/ML: LLM systems, RAG, fine-tuning, blockchain
Completed core coursework in software engineering, algorithms, databases, operating systems, cryptography, and neural networks.

references.name: Leonardo Velarde
references.reference: Waldo is an incredible generalist. He tried to challenge me technically in some projects and I learnt a lot for his high level. I wish I work with he in another organization as co-workers. I think It's a most impactful engineer I ever know, and as a person, I think I found a great friend, too.

references.name: Javier Casarrubias
references.reference: Waldo is a really professional and capable guy. He likes to learn new stuff, he's always learning new technologies, methodologies or anything in general, he always want to know more. He's also really responsible and always finishes what he started. He's also always helping people to improve and be better, he likes to teach and really smart in general.

references.name: David Delgado
references.reference:
Waldo it's a professional with really good skills. He persuaded me and inspired me to work on the best way through good practice and professionalism.

Waldo it's a co-worker who is always willing to help others co-workers and prepare them to grow professionally in many ways.

I found Waldo a very great person and an example to follow technically and personally.

references.name: Fernando Lazcano
references.reference: Waldo supported me and encouraged me to grow beyond engineering skills. He has a human part which allowed him to give me life tips. I really enjoyed the time we spent working and talking about life. He is the smartest person I have met, I am grateful for that.

projects.name: Average True Range In Ticks — TradingView Indicator
projects.description: This is trading indicator that shows you the volatility of current ticker.
highlights: This indicator allows you to select to visualize the ATR in either ticks or points.
Allows for multi-timeframe analysis by selecting a different timeframe than the chart's.

projects.name: Actionhero OAS Plugin
projects.description: Repository to auto-generate OpenAPI Specification documentation for Actionhero APIs.
highlights: Sets up a beautiful UI to document your API conforming to OAS (Swagger) specs.
